actions.name: Build Armbian SDK
actions.description: Builds and publishes Armbian SDK images for specified board and OS combinations, including preloaded development environments. Aggregates per-image asset manifests into a single JSON file for easier consumption. Ensures that the release is promoted to a full release only if all matrix builds succeed.
actions.cron_schedule: at 0 minutes past the hour, at 3:00

actions.name: Delete Old Releases
actions.description: Deletes old releases from the repository by identifying and removing both full releases and pre-releases that exceed a specified limit. It processes the releases based on their creation date, retaining the most recent three releases of each type. The operation is performed daily and can also be triggered manually.
actions.cron_schedule: at 0 minutes past the hour, at 3:00

actions.name: Dependency Review
actions.description: Scans dependency manifest files in pull requests to identify known-vulnerable package versions. Blocks merging of pull requests that introduce these vulnerabilities if the workflow run is marked as required.

actions.name: Dispatch download index update
actions.description: Triggers the regeneration of the download index for armbian.github.io after the successful completion of the Build Armbian SDK workflow or upon manual dispatch. It sends a repository dispatch event to update the download index with details about the source workflow and run. This ensures that the index is always in sync with the latest SDK release.

actions.name: Watchdog
actions.description: Polls the latest runs of specified workflows and reruns failed jobs if they have not exceeded the retry limit. This helps to automatically recover from transient failures without manual intervention. It targets workflows related to building the Armbian SDK and downloading infrastructure dispatch indices.

actions.name: Scorecard supply-chain security
actions.description: Runs security analysis on the codebase using the Scorecard tool, evaluating various security metrics. Publishes results to the OpenSSF REST API and uploads them to GitHub's code scanning dashboard. Optionally uploads the results as artifacts for further review.
actions.cron_schedule: at 20 minutes past the hour, at 7:00, on Tue
